title: heliopsis helianthoides subsp. scabra
intro: Heliopsis helianthoides subsp. scabra, also known as rough heliopsis, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), characterized by its rough, hairy leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of rough heliopsis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral activity against certain viral infections. Rough heliopsis can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or tea, and is often used in herbal remedies to support the immune system and promote overall health.
